project.name: Demo Application
project.description: Demo project for bug triage with GitHub Copilot SDK

bugs.summary: NullPointerException in UserAuthenticationService.authenticate()
bugs.description:
Users are encountering a NullPointerException when attempting to log in. The error occurs in UserAuthenticationService.authenticate() at line 142. Stack trace shows the user session object is null when the token validation succeeds but the session cache has expired.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Log in with valid credentials
2. Wait for session to expire (30 min)
3. Attempt any authenticated action
4. Login redirect triggers the NPE

Impact: ~200 users affected per day

bugs.summary: Memory leak in WebSocket connection handler
bugs.description:
The WebSocket connection handler in the real-time notification service is not properly cleaning up closed connections. Memory usage increases by ~50MB per hour under normal load. After 24 hours, the service OOMs and restarts.

Root cause appears to be in ConnectionPool.removeConnection() where event listeners are not being detached before removing the connection reference.

Monitoring alert: avg memory usage > 2GB threshold

bugs.summary: API rate limiter returns 500 instead of 429 when Redis is unavailable
bugs.description:
When the Redis instance backing the API rate limiter goes down, the rate limiting middleware throws an unhandled exception and returns HTTP 500 instead of gracefully degrading or returning HTTP 429.

This was discovered during a Redis failover event on 2024-03-01. All API requests returned 500 for approximately 3 minutes.

Expected behavior: Fall back to in-memory rate limiting or allow requests through with a warning log.

bugs.summary: Dashboard charts not rendering on Safari 17.x
bugs.description:
The analytics dashboard charts (built with D3.js) fail to render on Safari 17.x. The SVG elements are created but the paths are not drawn. Console shows 'TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading transform)' in chart-renderer.js.

Affects: Safari 17.0, 17.1, 17.2 on macOS Sonoma
Works fine on: Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari 16.x

bugs.summary: SQL injection vulnerability in search endpoint
bugs.description:
The /api/v2/search endpoint does not properly sanitize the 'query' parameter before passing it to the database query builder. A crafted search query can execute arbitrary SQL.

POC: GET /api/v2/search?query='; DROP TABLE users; --

This is a CRITICAL security vulnerability that needs immediate attention. The endpoint is publicly accessible without authentication.

bugs.summary: File upload fails silently for files > 10MB
bugs.description:
When users attempt to upload files larger than 10MB through the document management UI, the upload appears to complete successfully (progress bar reaches 100%) but the file is not actually stored. No error message is shown.

The issue is in the multipart upload handler which has a hardcoded 10MB limit that silently truncates the stream.

Expected: Show clear error message with file size limit, or support chunked uploads.

bugs.summary: Race condition in order processing causes duplicate charges
bugs.description:
Under high concurrency, the order processing service occasionally processes the same order twice, resulting in duplicate charges to customers. This happens when two instances of the order processor pick up the same message from the queue before the first one marks it as processed.

Occurrence rate: ~0.1% of orders during peak hours
Financial impact: ~$5,000/month in duplicate charges requiring manual refunds

bugs.summary: Elasticsearch index corruption after rolling restart
bugs.description:
After performing a rolling restart of the Elasticsearch cluster, some indices become corrupted with missing shards. The cluster health turns yellow and search results become incomplete.

Affected indices: products-*, user-activity-*
Cluster: production-es-cluster (3 nodes)

Workaround: Manually trigger shard reallocation via _cluster/reroute API

bugs.summary: Email notifications sent with wrong timezone offset
bugs.description:
Scheduled email notifications (daily digests, weekly reports) are being sent with UTC timestamps instead of the user's configured timezone. Users in PST see timestamps 8 hours ahead.

The bug is in NotificationScheduler.formatTimestamp() which ignores the user.timezone preference and defaults to UTC.

Affected: All users with non-UTC timezone settings (~60% of user base)

bugs.summary: Kubernetes pod CrashLoopBackOff due to health check timeout
bugs.description:
The payment-service pods are entering CrashLoopBackOff state during deployment. The liveness probe is configured with a 5-second timeout, but the service takes 8-10 seconds to initialize the database connection pool on cold start.

kubectl logs show: 'Connection pool initialization: waiting for 3 connections...'
Then the pod is killed by kubelet before initialization completes.

Fix: Increase initialDelaySeconds from 10 to 30, or add a startup probe.

bugs.summary: GraphQL N+1 query problem in user profile resolver
bugs.description:
The user profile GraphQL resolver fetches related entities (orders, addresses, preferences) with individual database queries per user, causing severe N+1 query performance issues when listing multiple users.

A query for 50 users triggers 150+ database queries. Page load time for admin user list: 12 seconds.

Solution: Implement DataLoader batching for user-related resolvers.

bugs.summary: CORS policy blocks requests from new subdomain
bugs.description:
After deploying the new marketing site to marketing.example.com, API requests from that subdomain are blocked by CORS policy. The Access-Control-Allow-Origin header only includes app.example.com and www.example.com.

Error in browser console: 'Access to XMLHttpRequest has been blocked by CORS policy: No Access-Control-Allow-Origin header'

Need to add marketing.example.com to the allowed origins list in api-gateway config.

bugs.summary: Password reset tokens not expiring after use
bugs.description:
Password reset tokens remain valid after being used to reset a password. A user (or attacker with access to the token) can reuse the same reset link multiple times to keep changing the password.

The token invalidation logic in PasswordResetService.completeReset() sets token.used=true but the validation check only looks at token.expiresAt.

Security impact: Medium-High - requires access to reset email

bugs.summary: CSV export truncates data at 65,536 rows
bugs.description:
When exporting large datasets to CSV, the export is silently truncated at 65,536 rows (the old Excel .xls row limit). Users downloading transaction reports for large date ranges get incomplete data without any warning.

The issue is in CsvExporter.writeRows() which uses a 16-bit integer for the row counter.

Expected: No artificial row limit for CSV exports, or at minimum a clear warning when data is truncated.

bugs.summary: Mobile app crashes on Android 14 when accessing camera
bugs.description:
The mobile application crashes with a SecurityException when attempting to access the camera on Android 14 devices. Android 14 introduced new photo/video permission model (READ_MEDIA_VISUAL_USER_SELECTED) that the app doesn't handle.

Crash report: SecurityException: Permission denied (missing CAMERA permission)
Affected: All Android 14+ users (~25% of Android user base)

Need to update targetSdk and implement the new photo picker API.

bugs.summary: Database connection pool exhaustion under load
bugs.description:
The user service database connection pool (HikariCP, max=20) is being exhausted during peak traffic, causing 'Connection is not available, request timed out after 30000ms' errors.

Metrics show connections are held for an average of 5 seconds due to a slow JOIN query in UserRepository.findWithPermissions(). During peak (500 req/s), all 20 connections are occupied.

Options: Increase pool size, optimize the query, or add caching.

bugs.summary: SSO login loop when IdP returns expired SAML assertion
bugs.description:
When the Identity Provider returns a SAML assertion with a NotOnOrAfter timestamp in the past (due to clock skew between IdP and SP), the application redirects back to the IdP, creating an infinite login loop.

Users see the login page flash repeatedly. The only workaround is clearing cookies.

Need to add clock skew tolerance (recommended: 2 minutes) to SAML assertion validation.

bugs.summary: Webhook delivery retries not respecting exponential backoff
bugs.description:
The webhook delivery system is supposed to retry failed deliveries with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s...) but all retries are happening at 1-second intervals. This is causing unnecessary load on recipient servers and triggering rate limiting.

Bug is in WebhookRetryPolicy.getNextDelay() where Math.pow(2, retryCount) is called but the result is not used - it always returns the base delay.

Affected: All webhook subscribers (~500 endpoints)

bugs.summary: Dark mode toggle doesn't persist across sessions
bugs.description:
When a user enables dark mode via the settings toggle, the preference is stored in component state but not persisted to localStorage or the user preferences API. On page refresh or new session, the app reverts to light mode.

Expected: Dark mode preference should persist across sessions via the user preferences API and fall back to localStorage.

bugs.summary: Audit log missing entries for bulk delete operations
bugs.description:
When users perform bulk delete operations (e.g., deleting multiple records from admin panel), only the first deletion is recorded in the audit log. The batch delete endpoint calls AuditLogger.log() inside a transaction that gets rolled back and re-executed for each item, but only commits the first log entry.

Compliance impact: SOC2 requirement for complete audit trail is not met for bulk operations.

tasks.description: Node.js 18 reaches EOL in April 2025. Need to upgrade all services to Node.js 20.x LTS. Requires testing all dependencies for compatibility.

tasks.summary: Implement structured logging with correlation IDs
tasks.description: Replace console.log statements with structured JSON logging. Add correlation ID propagation across microservices for distributed tracing.

stories.summary: As a user, I want to receive Slack notifications for critical alerts
stories.description: Integrate with Slack API to send real-time notifications when critical system alerts are triggered. Users should be able to configure which alert types they want to receive.

stories.summary: As an admin, I want to view system health dashboard
stories.description: Create a real-time dashboard showing system health metrics: uptime, error rates, response times, and resource utilization across all microservices.
